title: helichrysum italicum subsp. pseudolitoreum
intro: Helichrysum italicum subsp. pseudolitoreum, commonly known as the Italian strawflower or false strawflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a popular choice for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate health concerns such as arthritis, bronchitis, and digestive problems, and its active compounds have been shown to have antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Herbal preparations of Helichrysum italicum subsp. pseudolitoreum can be found in the form of essential oils, teas, and tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its health benefits.
